metadata.title: Frontend — LinkedIn OAuth & Account Settings UI

story.iWant: to see my linked providers in account settings and link/unlink them with a click
story.soThat: I can manage how I sign into Ditto
story.tasks: - [ ] Add LinkedIn button to `oauth-buttons.tsx` (AC: #1, #2) - [ ] Import LinkedIn icon from `@icons-pack/react-simple-icons` - [ ] Add LinkedIn `signIn('linkedin')` button with same styling as GitHub/Google - [ ] Verify LinkedIn provider is configured in `auth.ts` (add if missing) - [ ] Add LinkedIn provider to NextAuth config in `auth.ts` if not present (AC: #2) - [ ] Create `services/account-service.ts` (AC: #3-#8) - [ ] `getLinkedProviders()` — `GET /api/account/providers` - [ ] `linkProvider(data)` — `POST /api/account/link-provider` - [ ] `unlinkProvider(provider)` — `DELETE /api/account/providers/:provider` - [ ] `setPassword(password)` — `POST /api/account/set-password` - [ ] `changePassword(currentPassword, newPassword)` — `PUT /api/account/change-password` - [ ] Create `components/account/linked-providers.tsx` (AC: #3, #4, #5, #6) - [ ] Fetch and display linked providers on mount - [ ] Show provider icon + name + "Unlink" button for each linked provider - [ ] Show "Link" button for unlinked providers (GitHub, Google, LinkedIn) - [ ] Disable unlink button when only 1 method remains, show tooltip - [ ] Link flow: trigger OAuth signIn popup → on success call linkProvider API → refresh list → success toast - [ ] Unlink flow: confirmation dialog → call unlinkProvider API → refresh list → success toast - [ ] Error handling: display error toasts for 409 (already linked), 400 (last method) - [ ] Create `components/account/password-section.tsx` (AC: #7, #8) - [ ] Determine mode: "set" (no password) vs "change" (has password) based on provider list - [ ] Set mode: password + confirm fields, zod validation (min length), submit calls setPassword - [ ] Change mode: current password + new password + confirm fields, submit calls changePassword - [ ] Success: toast notification, reset form - [ ] Error: display validation errors inline, toast for server errors - [ ] Integrate components into account settings page (AC: #3, #7) - [ ] Add "Login Methods" card section with LinkedProviders component - [ ] Add "Password" card section with PasswordSection component - [ ] Write frontend tests (AC: #9) - [ ] Test LinkedProviders: renders linked providers, shows unlink buttons, disables last method unlink - [ ] Test LinkedProviders: shows link buttons for unlinked providers - [ ] Test PasswordSection: renders set-password form when no password, change-password form when has password - [ ] Test oauth-buttons: LinkedIn button renders on login/register pages

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
AC #1: Given the login and register pages, when rendered, then a LinkedIn OAuth button appears alongside GitHub and Google buttons with correct LinkedIn icon and styling.
AC #2: Given LinkedIn OAuth credentials are configured, when a user clicks the LinkedIn button on the login page, then the OAuth flow completes and the user is logged in / account is created.
AC #3: Given a user visits account settings, when the page loads, then they see a "Login Methods" section showing all linked providers with provider icon, name, and unlink button.
AC #4: Given a user clicks "Link Google" in account settings, when the OAuth popup completes, then Google appears in the linked providers list with a success toast notification.
AC #5: Given a user clicks "Unlink GitHub" and confirms in the confirmation dialog, when the request completes, then GitHub is removed from the list with a success toast.
AC #6: Given a user has only one auth method remaining, when viewing account settings, then the unlink button for that method is disabled with a tooltip "This is your only login method."
AC #7: Given an OAuth-only user (no password set), when they view account settings, then they see a "Set Password" form with password and confirm fields. Given a user with a password already set, then they see "Change Password" with current password, new password, and confirm fields.
AC #8: Given a user submits the set-password or change-password form with valid data, when the request succeeds, then a success toast appears and the form resets.
AC #9: Frontend tests cover LinkedProviders component rendering (linked/unlinked states, disabled unlink) and PasswordSection component rendering (set vs change mode).

doc.title: Multi-Provider Auth & Rate Limit Tuning Tech Spec
doc.section: Implementation Details — Phase 3: Frontend LinkedIn OAuth & Account Settings UI
doc.snippet: Contains UX/UI considerations, NextAuth integration details for link flow, component specifications, and accessibility requirements for the frontend implementation.

doc.title: Ditto Frontend Architecture
doc.section: Next.js App Router, Component Patterns, Service Layer
doc.snippet: Defines the App Router structure, shadcn/ui component conventions, react-hook-form + zod validation patterns, axios service layer, and sonner toast notification usage.

doc.title: Epic 8: Multi-Provider Authentication & Account Security
doc.section: Story 8.3: Frontend — LinkedIn OAuth & Account Settings UI
doc.snippet: Epic-level acceptance criteria and story map showing Story 8.3 depends on Story 8.2 (account management API). Defines LinkedIn OAuth, linked providers UI, and password management goals.

doc.title: Story 8.3: Frontend — LinkedIn OAuth & Account Settings UI
doc.section: Implementation Details, Key Code References, Context References
doc.snippet: Full story with tasks/subtasks, technical summary, project structure notes, and key code reference table mapping existing code locations to reuse patterns.

screen.name: Login
screen.description: Desktop login page with left branding panel and right form panel containing OAuth buttons (GitHub, Google, LinkedIn) and credential login form.

screen.name: Login - Tablet (768)
screen.description: Tablet-responsive login page with stacked layout.

screen.name: Settings
screen.description: Desktop settings page with sidebar and main content area. Contains LoginMethodsCard with providersList showing linked providers (icon, name, unlink button) and passwordSection.

screen.name: Settings - User Dropdown
screen.description: Desktop settings with user dropdown menu open.

screen.name: Settings - Delete Account
screen.description: Desktop settings with delete account confirmation dialog overlay.

screen.name: Settings - Set Password Modal
screen.description: Desktop settings with "Set Password" modal dialog open. Modal contains header, password fields (new password + confirm), and footer with cancel/save buttons. For OAuth-only users without a password.

screen.name: Settings - Change Password Modal
screen.description: Desktop settings with "Change Password" modal dialog open. Modal contains header, password fields (current password + new password + confirm), and footer with cancel/save buttons. For users with an existing password.

screen.name: Settings - Tablet (768)
screen.description: Tablet-responsive settings page with LoginMethodsCard containing providersList and passwordSection.

screen.name: Settings - Mobile (375)
screen.description: Mobile-responsive settings page with LoginMethodsCard containing providersList and passwordSection.

screen.name: Settings - Tablet - User Dropdown
screen.description: Tablet settings with user dropdown open and LoginMethodsCard visible.

screen.name: Settings - Mobile - User Dropdown
screen.description: Mobile settings with user dropdown open and LoginMethodsCard visible.

screen.name: Settings - Tablet - Delete Account
screen.description: Tablet settings with delete account dialog overlay.

screen.name: Settings - Mobile - Delete Account
screen.description: Mobile settings with delete account dialog overlay.

keyComponents.component: Contains providersList (linked provider rows with icon, name, unlink button), divider, and passwordSection (password status with set/change action).
Modal with header, body (password + confirm fields), footer (cancel + save buttons). Used for OAuth-only users setting their first password.
Modal with header, body (current password + new password + confirm fields), footer (cancel + save buttons). Used for users changing existing password.

file.reason: Existing OAuth button component rendering GitHub and Google buttons — extend with LinkedIn button using same pattern.
NextAuth configuration with GitHub, Google, and Credentials providers. Add LinkedIn provider. Reference signIn callback for link flow integration.
Service layer pattern to follow for new account-service.ts — uses axios instance with typed API calls.
Left panel branding component on login page — reference for layout context.
Shared auth input styling constant — reuse for consistency.

dependencies.note: LinkedIn provider may need to be installed if not bundled with next-auth. @icons-pack/react-simple-icons should already be installed for existing OAuth icons.

interface.name: GET /api/account/providers
interface.signature: GET /api/account/providers → { success: true, data: [{ auth_provider, avatar_url, created_at }] }

interface.name: POST /api/account/link-provider
interface.signature: POST /api/account/link-provider { provider, email, name, avatar_url } → { success: true, data: [providers] } | 409 conflict

interface.name: DELETE /api/account/providers/:provider
interface.signature: DELETE /api/account/providers/:provider → { success: true, data: [providers] } | 400 last method

interface.name: POST /api/account/set-password
interface.signature: POST /api/account/set-password { password } → { success: true, data: { message } } | 400 already set

interface.name: PUT /api/account/change-password
interface.signature: PUT /api/account/change-password { current_password, new_password } → { success: true, data: { message } } | 401 wrong password

interface.name: NextAuth signIn()
interface.signature: signIn(provider: string, options?: { redirect?: boolean, callbackUrl?: string }) → Promise

constraints.constraint: Follow existing shadcn/ui Card component pattern for Settings page sections
Use react-hook-form + zod for all form validation (password fields)
Use sonner toast() for success/error notifications
Use axios-based service layer pattern from auth-service.ts for API calls
OAuth link flow reuses NextAuth signIn() in popup mode, then calls backend link-provider endpoint
LinkedIn button must match existing GitHub/Google button styling in oauth-buttons.tsx
Responsive design must match Pencil designs: desktop (1440), tablet (768), mobile (375)
Password modals follow existing modal pattern (setPasswordModal, changePasswordModal from design)
Disable unlink when only 1 auth method remains — show tooltip explanation
Only add comments that explain WHY, not WHAT — code should be self-documenting
Prerequisite: Story 8.2 API endpoints must be available

tests.standards: Frontend tests use Vitest with React Testing Library. Mock API calls with vi.mock(). Test component rendering states and user interactions. Run with `pnpm test` from frontend directory.

ideas.idea: Test OAuthButtons renders LinkedIn button alongside GitHub and Google
Test LinkedProviders renders list of linked providers with icons and unlink buttons
Test LinkedProviders shows link buttons for unlinked providers
Test LinkedProviders disables unlink button when only one method linked, shows tooltip
Test LinkedProviders calls correct API on link/unlink actions
Test PasswordSection renders "Set Password" form when user has no password
Test PasswordSection renders "Change Password" form when user has password
Test PasswordSection shows success toast and resets form on successful submission
Test PasswordSection shows validation errors for invalid input
